prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read everything in module-4/policies/. Use those policy references to audit the

risks_fired.evidence: produce one row in a report: rule name... verdict (compliant / violating / 'I can't tell')
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body coaching 'I can't tell is a better answer than a confident guess' provides adequate mitigation; no change needed.

risks_fired.evidence: one line of evidence from my actual files for that verdict
risks_fired.fix_hint: Consider adding 'quote the exact sentence from the file that supports the verdict' to force real retrieval over plausible paraphrase.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I want to turn the useful parts of outputs/policy-report-raw.md into reusable se

risks_fired.evidence: Before you read or write any package files, ask me for 3-5 lines... Wait for my answer.
risks_fired.fix_hint: The ask IS the action here; preamble risk is real but low-impact — body coaching covers it.

risks_fired.evidence: Then propose the reusable package shape for my runtime and wait for me to steer.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body prose 'push back on anything that sounds like a generic GDPR brochure' adequately mitigates; no change needed.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Author the reusable security check now. Two lenses.  Build one personal skill so

risks_fired.evidence: Don't stop at one question. I'll tell you when enough is enough.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add inside fence: 'Ask one question, wait for my answer, then ask the next. Do not show me the full question list.' The body callout shape ('skim the questions') accepts the dump rather than mitigating it — does not satisfy the carve-out.

risks_fired.evidence: confirm this package-complete checklist: module-4/skills/security-audit/SKILL.md / any supporting reference files the SKILL.md requires
risks_fired.fix_hint: Move the attack-class verification cue inside the fence or add a body callout naming the failure mode (Claude confirms without student opening files) and giving a recovery push-back prompt.

risks_fired.evidence: Author the reusable security check now. Two lenses. [then] Before you save anything, grill me on missing details
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a body callout: 'Claude will summarise its approach briefly before the first question; skim past the opening paragraph and look for the question itself.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Install the authored skill at module-4/skills/security-audit/ as a personal Clau

risks_fired.evidence: Confirm the installed path so I can verify with ls.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body prose 'Run ls ~/.claude/skills/security-audit/ to confirm' provides adequate student-side verification.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Install the authored skill at module-4/skills/security-audit/ as a personal Clau

risks_fired.evidence: Confirm the installed path so I can verify with ls.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Desktop fork omits the 'Run ls to confirm' step present in the CLI fork; minor parity gap worth fixing.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Use your skill-creator skill to turn the authored source at module-4/skills/secu

risks_fired.evidence: Draft it, then surface the save action so I can save it.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Housekeeping step; body explains the Cowork sandbox mechanic adequately.

maintainer_acceptance.note: Prompt 3 question-dump (high) is accepted-by-design — the prompt explicitly multi-rounds ('Don't stop at one question'), body line 47 frames the wide grill as deliberate, body line 51 teaches the dump-and-handle pattern. Forcing one-at-a-time contradicts the pedagogy.
